heading: Security with Azure Managed Identities
body:
Workload Identity Federation with Azure Managed Identities provides the most secure way to
authenticate to MongoDB Atlas clusters running on Azure. This approach offers
the following security advantages:

- Leverages Managed Identities: This allows your applications running on Azure
  services (like Azure VMs, AKS, Azure Functions) to authenticate to MongoDB Atlas
  using their assigned Azure Managed Identities without needing to
  manage separate MongoDB credentials.

- Secure "Passwordless" Authentication: Your applications obtain short-lived
  OAuth 2.0 access tokens from the Azure metadata server, which MongoDB Atlas
  then verifies. This eliminates the need to store and manage sensitive database
  usernames and passwords within your application code or configurations.

- Centralized Identity Management: You manage the identities of your Azure
  workloads within the Azure Entra ID framework, providing a single
  source of truth for identity management.

- Enhanced Auditing: Azure provides audit logs for service account activity,
  enhancing traceability and security monitoring capabilities.

For the best security to access MongoDB Atlas clusters, you can combine
Workload Identity Federation with [Azure Private Link](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/services/private-link/).
This setup leverages the security and management capabilities of Azure,
while ensuring [private and secure network connectivity](private-endpoint-concepts)
to your database.
